title: A comprehensive atlas of the bonobo gut bacteriome and its associated host and exposome factors.
ehr_evidence: The study uses fecal samples and host/exposome data from wild and zoo-housed bonobos, not EHR.
summary: A comprehensive atlas of the gut bacteriome and associated host and exposome factors in 212 wild and zoo-housed bonobos (Pan paniscus).
key_findings: Differences in gut bacterial diversity between wild and captive bonobos.
Social contact is a stronger predictor of bacteriome similarity than genetics or other exposome factors.
Maternal effects persist even in non-cohabiting mother-offspring dyads.
exposure_domain: Exposome (diet, medication use, social contact, early life adversity, transit time)
data_availability_statement: Sequencing data have been deposited at the European Nucleotide Archive under project ENA: PRJEB86099 and are publicly available as of the date of publication. Pseudonymized sample data have been deposited and are publicly available as of the data of publication at Zenodo: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.14913431. All original code has been deposited and is publicly available as of the date of publication at Zenodo: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.14911049.
